decision-tree.root-question: Is the Django service reachable from the public internet (or a partially-trusted intranet)?

branch.conclusion: Skip the full baseline. Apply rule r5 only (env-loaded secrets + SECRET_KEY check) — every Django service deserves that. Stop here.

branch.question: Does the service expose authentication endpoints (login, password reset, registration, token issue)?
Is the service behind a trusted reverse proxy / CDN whose X-Forwarded-For semantics are documented?

branch.conclusion: Apply rules r1, r3, r4, r5. Skip r2 (no auth surface). HSTS ramp still mandatory.
Apply rules r1, r3, r4, r5. Document proxy assumptions before enabling any IP-based protections later.

branch.question: Is the proxy chain configuration verified (AXES_IPWARE_PROXY_COUNT correct)?

branch.conclusion: Verify proxy chain in staging FIRST. Without correct trust config, axes/ratelimit will lock out all users. Apply rules r1/r3/r4/r5 immediately; defer r2 until proxy chain is verified.
Full baseline. Apply all five rules. Schedule HSTS ramp per `05-examples.xml`.

example.trace: public DRF API + login endpoint + behind Cloudflare with documented X-Forwarded-For → q1=yes → q2=yes → q4=yes → full baseline (r1+r2+r3+r4+r5)
